title: Make link TX sequence allocation atomic per context
description: Trigger: two threads call lichen_link_tx()/lichen_link_next_seq() on the same lichen_link_ctx. lichen/subsys/lichen/link/link_ctx.c updates ctx->tx_seq and ctx->epoch without synchronization around line 163. Impact: concurrent TX can allocate duplicate (epoch, seqnum) pairs. When ctx->has_link_key is true, duplicate pairs reuse the AES-CCM nonce built from eui64, epoch, and seqnum, breaking link-layer authentication guarantees. Add per-context locking or an atomic sequence allocation contract.
close_reason: Implemented fixes in assigned subagents and ran available focused checks; full Zephyr builds remain blocked by existing workspace/module setup issues.
